items.briefing:
The Australian Government has launched its Horizon 2 Cyber Security Action Plan, signalling continued investment in national cyber resilience — good news for registered training organisations that handle sensitive student data. Separately, Scamwatch is warning Australians about two active scam types: food delivery scams targeting everyday shoppers, and fake online relationships designed to build trust before stealing money. Both are increasingly sophisticated and can affect staff in their personal lives as much as at work.

On a broader note, a new AI model called Claude Fable 5 has raised cybersecurity concerns, as experts warn it could make it easier for scammers to craft convincing phishing messages. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected message — whether by email, text, or social media — that asks you to click a link or share personal details, stop and verify through an official channel before responding.

featured.title: Government launches Horizon 2 action plan for Australia’s cyber security strategy
featured.quote: Australia's federal government launches Horizon 2 cyber security strategy, shaping national defense priorities.
